When they confront these opportunities or when we create these opportunities for them is they want to be cheap about it How little can I give them? How little can I do for them? Well first of all they rarely make the comparison to the cost of acquiring one on their own They don't even really most of them don't even know that number and It's a vital number to know for all sorts of reasons, but it's really germane to this conversation And I will and I have had these conversations. Well, let's figure out exactly what it costs you to get one on your own Right, so here's your budget for all your advertising you did last year. Here's what you spent on that Here's what you spent running around on airplanes do here's what you spent on that Here's what you spent at the trade shows we added all up. We divided by the number of customers you got what did you spend? 2300 dollars, right and you don't want to give this guy any more than 600 Why? Let's give him three thousand Because he's already got them all in one place we can get a whole bunch of them in a hurry time has value Right. Well, certainly let's give him 2300 If you really want to feel emotionally good about this like you really got a great deal Give him 2999 dollars and 99 cents so it costs you a penny less than getting it on your own But for God's sakes, let's not argue over 600 to 800 You know, let's give him a guarantee Let's write him on a check that he keeps no matter what what I just solved that for a client the other day two weeks ago Guys on a coaching call with me. He's got three joint venture partners. They're having a money conversation He wants he's got a system in place that works. He wants to email their list. I Said are you convinced you're convinced these lists are winners? They're winners and I can't get in For all for all good reasons. They're all smart marketers. They all got other stuff. They're doing with their list His promotion gets in a way of their promotion They might be trading a buck for 50 cents because their customer buys his thing and then they don't have enough money to buy Their own thing so they traded away all reasonable good I said, here's what you did By the way, not original with me This is how Halbert used to buy newspaper space at a disc discount. Yeah, and you've probably all heard that story All right, it's very hard for anybody to send back a check There's a visceral emotional reaction to it people can't they hate it They just Set it aside They give it to the secretary and say send this and then they go back out and get it back You know It's like me with a donut. I'll throw it in a waste basket, but I'll go get it So you got to smash it and you got it preferably you don't get it at all in the first place right so Checks are really hard to give back. They just it's painful. I said FedEx them checks Say here's we're here's all the money we're gonna make together and here's all the wonderful shit We're gonna do and here's an advance of $25,000 and it's yours to keep no matter what Three FedEx is three deals Boom Right, they can't stand it. It took one two days. It took one two days. The other ones were like 1002 you know, I mean I said be I said be by the phone, you know cuz They're probably gonna open that baby And the other one took two days, but still three for three, right? Well now what happens if one of those lists sucks? Let's say he's 33 percent wrong. So what? There is a man Yeah, Joe gonna be great. He's not gonna be a hundred percent wrong The way the math works. He only needs one of the three to work or all three of them to work The tenth of what he thinks they're good with our main evidence and so the money's right. So you don't want to be cheap You don't want to There are people who won't go at meetings in order to make these things happen I can assure you face-to-face beats anything Beats anything So if it's important and it's really really valuable get your ass on a plane
